title: new TypeORM DataSource with ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the database TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The TypeORM DataSource validates the database server certificate (ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new DataSource is created with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the database server's TLS certificate for every connection in the pool. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database — exposing every query, credential, and row. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the provider's CA via ssl.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No DataSource construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The DataSource does not set ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted into a TypeORM DataSource to connect to a managed Postgres/MySQL without the CA. It ships and disables MITM protection on the database.
